title: Context
description: Skip props with useContext — and its trade-offs.

options: Values needed by many components at different depths (theme, user, i18n)
questions.explanation: Rarely-changing global-ish values are the sweet spot.

questions.prompt: Downside of putting everything in one context:
options: Every consumer re-renders when ANY part of the value changes

questions.explanation: React 19 allows using the Context object directly as the provider.

options: Reads the CURRENT value from the nearest provider above
questions.explanation: Component subscribes and re-renders when the provider's value changes.

options: use(context) — a hook that works in more places, including conditionally
questions.explanation: use() reads a context or a Promise; can be called inside conditions/loops.
